Auckland Uni's Game Dev Club

Game Developer Guild!

Est. 2018

Intro

The University of Auckland Game Developers Guild is an inclusive community dedicated to teaching students and hobbyists the core aspects of game development.

We run tutorials throughout the semester covering art, code and game design, then put those skills to the test in our semester-long GDG Jam.

Our philosophy

Our philosophy

(corporate lingo ofc)

That’s what GDG is about: bring an idea, pick up new skills, and make something. Art, code, music, design, everything belongs here! You don’t need to know everything before you start as the fun is in figuring it out along the way.

Games are better with people, so our events are built around meeting teammates, sharing what you’re working on, and playing each other's games. Teaming up is encouraged, never forced, and everyone is welcome, even if you’re shy.

FAQ

I don’t know how to code!

If you don’t know how to code, that’s okay! One of our core workshops is teaching members how to code so you can come along there to learn, otherwise our Discord server has resources on some of the fundamentals of game development.

What if I’m non-UOA?

You can still join! Several of our long-lasting members are graduates, non-UOA Students, industry workers, and more. We’re open to anyone and everyone, as long as you follow The Code (of Conduct), and love games and gamedev!

Help, I’m a total newbie…

Even if you’re ABSOLUTELY new to game development, our community is open for all to ask questions and help each other in any way.
